title: Governed Agent Spend — response envelope
description: Machine-checkable definition of the normative wire format in spec §3 (https://sproutpad.ai/spec.md). A response body is conformant iff it validates against exactly one of the two branches: a success envelope or an error envelope. Field names are normative — no renaming, no restyling.

successEnvelope.description: Success. Read-only responses carry any shape under `data`; responses whose operation contract promises a continuation must additionally satisfy #/$defs/actionableSuccessEnvelope, and successful public MUTATION operations must satisfy #/$defs/mutatingSuccessEnvelope (§3.1). Whether an operation is mutating is not inferable from the body alone, so graders apply the operation-specific definition to responses of known operations.

replayed.description: Transport metadata. True only when an Idempotency-Key replay returned the stored response without re-executing the mutation.

actionableSuccessEnvelope.description: A successful operation that is contractually required to teach the next step. Fake-money sandbox control-protocol transitions use this definition because they cannot mutate a provider, customer resource, or real payment instrument; a §3.1 public mutation must instead satisfy mutatingSuccessEnvelope.

mutatingSuccessEnvelope.description: The complete §3.1 envelope for every successful public mutation: governance evidence inside data and a non-empty nextActions array at the top level.

mutatingSuccessData.description: The §3.1 contract for the `data` object of a successful public mutation: receipt, undo, and budgetRemainingUsd. nextActions belongs only at the top level of the envelope.

receipt.description: What happened: the action, its costs, and provider references for everything created (§3.1).

undo.description: How to reverse the mutation: a concrete command/call with irreversible:false, or the explicit object {command:null, irreversible:true}. A bare null is invalid (§3.1).

nextActions.description: Ordered sensible next calls (§3.1). Array order is execution order. When authority changes or a precondition is required, the instruction names the actor and the required endpoint, header, or ordering constraint; clients must not infer omitted authority.

actionableNextActions.description: A non-empty continuation list required by actionableSuccessEnvelope.

errorEnvelope.description: Error (§3.2). blockedBy names the specific gate in a namespaced vocabulary. resolution, when present, is the machine-readable legitimate path forward; when absent, no legitimate path exists.

blockedBy.description: Namespaced gate identifier, e.g. policy:max_domain_price, budget:exceeded, input:invalid_domain. Values (not field names) use lower snake_case.

resolution.description: The legitimate path forward. `type` names the path; the remaining fields carry the artifact needed to walk it (a task id to poll, a URL to hand to the human, a hint for a retry).

type.description: e.g. human_approval, retry, signup, authenticate, update_billing, upgrade, unfreeze, human_action, human_dashboard
